_meta.description: Per-park physical metadata used by Wizard v3.1 batting-prop features.

fields.cf_azimuth_deg: Bearing from home plate toward the center-field point, degrees clockwise from true north. Source: publicly documented stadium orientations; values are nominal (±2°).
fields.altitude_ft: Field-level elevation above sea level, feet.
fields.wind_sensitivity_tier: extreme | high | medium | low | dome — how much wind affects total bases relative to baseline.
fields.wind_mult_out: TB multiplier when wind blows OUT (aligned within 45° of CF azimuth). Applied on top of pull_side_wind_vector feature.
fields.wind_mult_in: TB multiplier when wind blows IN (opposing CF azimuth). Negative impact on power numbers.
fields.wind_mult_cross: TB multiplier for crosswind (90° offset from CF). Moderate effect.
fields.wind_sensitivity_note: Human-readable explanation of park-specific wind behavior.

wind_tier_definitions.extreme: 3x out / 2.5x in. Fully exposed ballpark with strong wind channel effects (Wrigley, Fenway).
wind_tier_definitions.high: 2x out / 1.5x in. Open exposed park with consistent wind influence.
wind_tier_definitions.medium: 1.5x out / 1x in. Standard open park, moderate wind effect.
wind_tier_definitions.low: 1.2x out / 0.8x in. Suppressed wind pocket, marine layer, or sheltered bowl geometry.
wind_tier_definitions.dome: 0x. Wind irrelevant — always override wind features to 0.

ARI.wind_sensitivity_note: Retractable roof closed for most afternoon games. Wind irrelevant when closed; medium when open.

ATL.wind_sensitivity_note: Bowl-shaped suburban park with moderate wind exposure. ENE orientation aligns with summer thermals. Baseline sensitivity.

BAL.wind_sensitivity_note: Inner-harbor location. Prevailing SW wind (bearings 200-250) hits CF obliquely — mild push toward RF. Sea-level density keeps air slightly heavier.

BOS.wind_sensitivity_note: Compact urban park with no wind breaks. Charles River corridor funnels NE winds directly toward CF/RF. Short right-field dimensions amplify crosswind impact on HR rates significantly. Second only to Wrigley for wind volatility.

CHC.wind_sensitivity_note: Wrigley_Out: Lake Michigan wind corridor creates the most wind-sensitive environment in MLB. CF faces nearly due North (355°). South wind (bearing ~180°) = tailwind OUT — dramatically inflates HR and TB. North wind = fierce headwind IN — suppresses scoring dramatically. A 10mph OUT wind here equals ~30mph elsewhere for TB impact. Flag as Wrigley_Out when wind_bearing 150-210.
CHC.notes: CF nearly due N; wind off Lake Michigan dominates

CHW.wind_sensitivity_note: South Side exposed urban park. Lake winds from the NE (bearing 30-60) align closely with CF (40°) creating strong OUT conditions. Similar corridor to Wrigley but less pronounced due to lower seating bowl.

CIN.wind_sensitivity_note: Ohio River valley creates channeled SW winds. Moderate exposed location. NNE CF orientation slightly aided by spring thermal uplift from river.

CLE.wind_sensitivity_note: Lake Erie corridor. Due-North CF (0°) aligns with south lake wind (bearing 180-200) for powerful OUT conditions in warm months. Cold lake air increases density when wind is off the lake, suppressing HR in early season.

COL.wind_sensitivity_note: Altitude_Dominant: At 5200ft, thin air (rho ~1.03 kg/m3) is the primary driver — NOT wind. Wind multiplier intentionally suppressed vs other open parks. The altitude effect is baked into air_density_rho; do not double-count wind on top of it. Apply altitude boost separately.

DET.wind_sensitivity_note: Large park with deep CF (420ft) partially mitigates OUT wind effect on HR. Wind sensitivity is baseline medium.

HOU.wind_sensitivity_note: Retractable roof closed most games due to Houston heat/humidity. Wind irrelevant when closed. Override all wind features to 0 unless confirmed open.

KC.wind_sensitivity_note: Exposed suburban stadium. Kansas plains wind patterns create variable but moderate effects. The water feature beyond CF creates slight cooling/dampening effect.

LAA.wind_sensitivity_note: Orange County onshore breezes generally mild and steady. Semi-enclosed by rock formations and berm. Low wind sensitivity — wind rarely a decisive factor.

LAD.wind_sensitivity_note: Chavez Ravine bowl geometry suppresses surface winds significantly. Marine layer from the Pacific creates dense, cool air that counteracts OUT winds. Wind rarely changes scoring dynamics here.

MIA.wind_sensitivity_note: Roof almost always closed due to Miami humidity/heat/rain. Wind irrelevant.

MIL.wind_sensitivity_note: Retractable roof. When open (comfortable weather), medium sensitivity applies — ENE orientation catches southerly winds. Model as dome unless confirmed open.

MIN.wind_sensitivity_note: Unusual due-East CF (90°). West wind (bearing ~270°) = strong tailwind OUT toward CF. Minneapolis exposed downtown location with few wind breaks. Cold early-season air (dense) partially offsets wind OUT effect.

NYM.wind_sensitivity_note: Flushing Meadows exposed location. Atlantic maritime winds (SE/SW) create moderate-to-strong effects. Adjacent to water — humidity increases air density slightly, partially offsetting OUT wind.

NYY.wind_sensitivity_note: South Bronx exposed location. ENE-facing CF (75°) is hit by Hudson River corridor southerly winds. Short porch in right field amplifies crosswind effect on pull-side HR. High sensitivity especially for LHB pulling into short porch.

OAK.wind_sensitivity_note: Legacy venue. San Francisco Bay cold marine layer creates consistently dense air. East Bay wind patterns mild and offshore. Low TB impact from wind.

ATH.wind_sensitivity_note: Athletics temporary Sacramento home. Central Valley location with afternoon delta breeze from the SW — can push toward CF in summer. Medium sensitivity.

PHI.wind_sensitivity_note: South Philly exposed suburban park. Delaware Valley wind patterns moderate. NNE CF orientation means southerly summer winds are a tailwind OUT. Medium baseline.

PIT.wind_sensitivity_note: Atypical ESE CF orientation (118°) overlooking Allegheny River. NW winds (bearing 315-330) align as tailwind OUT toward CF — Pittsburgh prevailing wind direction. River valley creates a wind tunnel effect. High sensitivity, directionally unusual.

SD.wind_sensitivity_note: Oracle_Cousin — similar to Oracle Park. Consistent Pacific marine layer creates cold dense air. Downtown San Diego sea breeze from the SW is mild. Park's deep CF suppresses HR. Low wind sensitivity overall.

SF.wind_sensitivity_note: Oracle_Dead_Pocket: The famous 'dead pocket' in the right-center gap is created by wind off San Francisco Bay (generally from the W/NW) being deflected by AT&T Park's architecture. Due-East CF (90°) faces the bay, but the cold dense marine air and the swirling pocket reduce the effective OUT wind impact by ~40% vs a naive vector calculation. Cross-bay wind increases air density, partially SUPPRESSING power despite the apparent OUT direction. Flag as Oracle_Dead_Pocket when wind_bearing 270-330 — reduced, not enhanced.

SEA.wind_sensitivity_note: Retractable roof. Seattle rain/cold means roof commonly closed. Wind irrelevant when closed.

STL.wind_sensitivity_note: Mississippi River corridor creates channeled NW-SE winds. NE CF aligns moderately with summer southerly winds. Standard medium sensitivity.

TB.wind_sensitivity_note: Fixed dome. Wind fully irrelevant. All wind features must be zeroed out for this park.
TB.notes: Dome — wind vector inert; verify 2026 venue after roof damage

TEX.wind_sensitivity_note: Fully enclosed retractable roof (always closed for air conditioning in Texas heat). Wind irrelevant. +5.55r park factor outlier driven by dimensions/humidity, NOT wind.

TOR.wind_sensitivity_note: Retractable roof; Toronto climate means roof typically closed. Wind irrelevant when closed.

WSH.wind_sensitivity_note: Anacostia River valley, open air. NNE CF aligns with Potomac summer southerlies — moderate OUT conditions in summer. Sea-level dense air. Baseline medium sensitivity.
